events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes EMT (Electrical Metallic Tubing) with a kinked/flattened bend. This is a defect. NEC Article 358 covers EMT installation. Kinked or flattened bends reduce the internal diameter and can damage conductor insulation during pulling. NEC 358.24 requires bends to be made without damage to the raceway and without substantially reducing the internal diameter. A kinked/flattened bend is out of tolerance and non-compliant. The work is rough-complete. Decision should be fail. Risk is medium/high because it can damage wire insulation and impede pulling. Let's say high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 85. Value: maybe percent reduction in diameter? The task says "Estimate or interpret the measurable condition and state why it is out of tolerance." A kinked/flattened bend likely reduces internal diameter significantly, maybe >10% reduction. I'll set value to something like 15 (percent reduction) or just 0 if not asked. But value is for numeric reading. I'll estimate a flattening reducing diameter by ~20%. Let's use value: 20. Confidence 0.8.
